claim: For any agent with finite epistemic capacity r in an environment with irreversible terminal sinks C where environmental reach R > r, there exists an informational horizon beyond which survival cannot be guaranteed without interactive state expansion.
assumptions: A1: Epistemic capacity r is finite and bounded.
A2: Terminal sinks C ⊂ X are absorbing and irrecoverable within energy budget E.
A3: Environmental informational reach R > r.
A4: Survival in adaptive environments requires agent simulation depth D_a > environment reaction depth D_e.
falsifiers: A finite-r agent achieving P_s = 1 in an environment satisfying A1–A4.
Proof that informational scarcity does not convert error probability to deterministic terminal failure.
